challenged_findings.challenge: Is the 5-day overage within any tolerance? Is there any exemption from authorization policy? Could year-end rules interact?
challenged_findings.resolution: Finding confirmed. No tolerance or exemption applies.

supporting_evidence: Record R900006 detail confirms: expense_date=2025-03-01, reimburse_date=2025-05-05
expense_date is March, not December: Article 9 year-end rule does not apply
Policy version: 2025 revision effective 2025-01-01, applies to all 2025 records
No other policy provides an exemption for this overdue record
opposing_evidence: Article 7 uses '原则上' (in principle), not absolute prohibition — but standard audit interpretation treats it as mandatory
Delay is only 5 days over the 60-day limit

exceptions_or_boundaries.60-day boundary: 65 days exceeds 60-day limit by exactly 5 days. No rounding or tolerance clause in policy.
exceptions_or_boundaries.year_end_exclusion: Record is March, not December. Article 9 does not apply.

rationale: R900006 is a confirmed overweight overdue reimbursement. All three boundary checks (60-day, year-end, special_approval) return negative—no applicable exemption. The 5-day overage does not qualify for any policy exemption under the 2025 revision.
